title: Announcing Lingmo OS 1.0 — A New Dawn for Linux
author: Lingmo Team
excerpt: After years of development, we are thrilled to announce the first stable release of Lingmo OS.
content:
We are proud to announce the first stable release of Lingmo OS. Built on Debian 13 with a custom desktop environment, Lingmo OS brings a fresh, modern experience to Linux users worldwide.

## Key Features

- **Lingmo Desktop Environment**: A modern, intuitive desktop built with Qt Quick and tailored for productivity.
- **Wayland by Default**: Experience smooth, secure graphics with Wayland as the default display server.
- **Performance Optimizations**: Boot times under 5 seconds on modern hardware, with minimal memory usage.
- **Enhanced Security**: Full disk encryption, secure boot support, and regular security updates.

## Getting Started

Download the ISO from our [download page](/download) and follow the [installation guide](/docs/install) to get started.

title: Lingmo Desktop: A Deep Dive into Our Custom Desktop Environment
author: Lingmo Team
excerpt: Explore the architecture and design philosophy behind the Lingmo Desktop Environment.
content:
The Lingmo Desktop Environment (LingmoDE) is the heart of the Lingmo OS experience. Built from the ground up using Qt Quick and the LingmoUI toolkit, it delivers a cohesive and elegant user interface.

## Design Philosophy

LingmoDE follows three core principles:

1. **Simplicity**: Every element serves a purpose. No clutter, no confusion.
2. **Consistency**: A unified design language across all system applications.
3. **Performance**: Smooth animations and fast response times, even on older hardware.

## Architecture

The desktop environment consists of several key components:

- **Shell**: The main desktop shell managing windows, workspaces, and the global menu.
- **Dock**: A macOS-style dock for launching and switching applications.
- **Control Center**: Centralized settings for system configuration.
- **Notifications**: A unified notification system with Do Not Disturb support.

title: Security First: How Lingmo OS Protects Your Data
author: Lingmo Team
excerpt: Learn about the security features built into Lingmo OS.
content:
Security is a top priority for Lingmo OS. We believe that users should have full control over their data and privacy.

## Built-in Security Features

- **Full Disk Encryption**: LUKS-based encryption during installation to protect your data at rest.
- **Secure Boot**: Support for UEFI Secure Boot to prevent unauthorized code from running during startup.
- **Firewall**: Pre-configured firewall with sensible defaults.
- **Regular Updates**: Security patches delivered through automatic updates.

## Privacy

Lingmo OS does not collect telemetry or user data. We respect your privacy by default, with no phone-home features enabled out of the box.

title: Our Journey to Wayland: Why We Chose the Future of Display Servers
author: Lingmo Team
excerpt: Why Lingmo OS uses Wayland as the default display server.
content:
Wayland is the next-generation display protocol for Linux, designed to replace the aging X11 system. Lingmo OS has adopted Wayland as the default display server from the start.

## Why Wayland?

- **Security**: Wayland's design prevents keyloggers and screen scrapers by isolating windows.
- **Performance**: Direct buffer management reduces latency and improves frame rates.
- **Modern Architecture**: Simplified driver model with better support for modern graphics hardware.

## Current Status

Wayland support in Lingmo OS is mature and production-ready. All LingmoDE components are fully Wayland-native, providing a smooth and secure desktop experience.
